title: Delicate Dry Ethiopian Herring Ceviche
description: A funky, crisp, sick‑day Ethiopian dish featuring herring cured with shio‑koji and citrus, no onion or garlic, served on a tagliere.
instructions: Rinse the herring fillets under cold running water and pat dry with paper towels.
In a shallow bowl, whisk together shio‑koji, lemon juice, lime juice, chili flakes, and seaweed flakes until well blended.
Slice the herring into bite‑size cubes and place them into the citrus‑koji mixture, ensuring all pieces are coated.
Cover the bowl and refrigerate for 30 minutes to allow the flavors to infuse and the herring to firm up.
After 30 minutes, remove the herring cubes, drain excess liquid, and gently pat them dry with a clean towel.
Transfer the cured herring onto a clean tagliere and sprinkle with toasted sesame seeds, chopped cilantro, and a pinch of freshly ground black pepper.
Drizzle a thin line of extra‑virgin olive oil over the top for added sheen (optional).
Garnish with an additional sprinkle of chili flakes for heat.
Let the dish rest at room temperature for 5 minutes to allow flavors to meld before serving.
Present immediately on the tagliere, accompanied by crisp cucumber ribbons or a side of lightly roasted injera for authenticity.
